item.title: Banks Line Up €1.7 Billion of Debt for Triton’s Flender Buyout
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. are among banks that have lined up around €1.7 billion ($1.97 billion) of debt financing that will back Triton Partners’ acquisition of German gearbox-maker Flender GmbH.

item.title: Indian Developer Prestige Plans $6.3 Billion in Project Launches
item.description: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd., which counts BlackRock Inc. and Vanguard Group Inc. among its investors, is targeting about 600 billion rupees ($6.3 billion) in estimated sales value for this financial year as the developer prepares to launch projects across the country, its billionaire founder said.

item.title: SpaceX Prepares for Debut as $75 Billion IPO Breaks Record
item.description: SpaceX made history with a $75 billion IPO that instantly turned it into one of the biggest public companies in the world. Now it has to win over the market.

item.title: Germany to Grow in 2026 as Spending Offsets War, Bundesbank Says
item.description: Germany’s economy will recover more slowly than expected because of the Iran war but pick up steam in the coming years thanks to fiscal stimulus, according to the Bundesbank.

item.title: Scammers Used Gemini AI to Help Build Spam Messages, Google Says
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google filed a lawsuit against a suspected Chinese cybercrime operation, alleging that the group used artificial intelligence to send more than 2 million text messages with fraudulent links attempting to scam cellphone users.

item.title: Britons See Inflation Hitting Double BOE Target, Poll Finds
item.description: Britons expect inflation to accelerate sharply in the coming year while businesses are turning more pessimistic, according to two Bank of England surveys that highlight the growing trade-off posed by the Iran war.

item.title: European Bonds Surge as Trump Touts Progress on Iran Peace Deal
item.description: European bonds rallied as oil prices slid on the renewed potential for a peace deal in the Middle East.

item.title: Warburg Pincus Is Said to Near Acquisition of Japanese Housing Firm JSB
item.description: Warburg Pincus is nearing a deal to buy Japanese housing firm JSB Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: ECB’s Moulin Sees Broadening Energy Shock, But Not In Wages Yet
item.description: The jump in oil prices has started to feed to other goods and services, even if euro-area wages aren’t yet affected, European Central Bank Governing Council member Emmanuel Moulin said.

item.title: How a Vibecoded Newsletter Is Making the Hay Market More Transparent
item.description: A conversation with HayWire's Aiden Johnson.

item.title: Odd Lots: Making the Hay Market More Transparent (Podcast)
item.description: The hay market is not a transparent market: It is very fractured by types of hay, whether it is alfalfa or clover hay. There are a few opaque, illiquid markets like this — scrap metal for instance — that require some hands-on investigating to figure out. Aiden Johnson is co-founder and CEO of the HayWire newsletter, which aims to make the hay market more transparent: He and co-founder Cole Glasgow use an AI model to mine public data sources — like USDA reports on auction prices across different

item.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Executives Plan to Attend G7 Summit
item.description: Artificial intelligence executives from Anthropic PBC, OpenAI and Alphabet Inc.’s Google are slated to attend the Group of Seven summit, a gathering of world leaders that gets underway next week in France.

item.title: ENN Is Said to Mull Dropping $12 Billion Buyout of Energy Unit
item.description: ENN Energy Holdings Ltd.’s biggest shareholder is considering abandoning a nearly $12 billion buyout offer for the Hong Kong-listed company, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Several Indonesian Bond Funds Delay Redemptions After Turmoil
item.description: Two funds that hold Indonesian bonds are delaying redemptions to manage liquidity pressures stemming from the nation’s recent financial market turmoil.

item.title: UK Tech Secretary Vows More Action on Pushing Funds to Startups
item.description: The UK’s Labour government will strengthen efforts to drive institutional funds into British tech companies, Technology Secretary Liz Kendall said, as she pledged more backing for the country’s artificial intelligence sector.

item.title: SpaceX’s $100 Billion Order Book Is Tiny Versus Hot Chinese IPOs
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX made history with the biggest initial public offering of all time, but there’s at least one metric where the deal falls short.

item.title: Telefónica Weighs Ending Movistar Cycling Team Sponsorship
item.description: Telefónica SA is looking to cut sponsorship of a road cycling team in the sport’s premier championship as part of a broad review of marketing spending, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US LNG Exporters Find Few Takers in Europe for New Supply
item.description: US liquefied natural gas developers expected Europe to become the cornerstone market for the next generation of export projects. Instead, the region is increasingly shying away from the long-term contracts needed to get them built.

item.title: Italian Financial Stocks Soar as Paschi Bids Revive Deal Chatter
item.description: Italian banks and insurers sprang into life this week as a potential bidding battle for Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA triggered broad gains across the country’s financial stocks.

item.title: Kioxia Becomes Japan’s Most Valuable Firm as AI Mania Goes On
item.description: Memory chipmaker Kioxia Holdings Corp. replaced Toyota Motor Corp. to become Japan’s largest company by market value, underscoring how the global artificial intelligence boom is reshaping the country’s corporate landscape.

item.title: FanDuel Owner Plans to Delist From London After Shift to US
item.description: Flutter Entertainment Plc announced plans to delist from the London Stock Exchange about two years after the online betting group shifted its primary listing to New York, the latest company to abandon the UK bourse to prioritize US trading.

item.title: Pakistan Budget Tests Growth Push Against IMF Constraints
item.description: Pakistan’s budget will test whether the government can revive growth without jeopardizing the fiscal gains supporting its economic recovery, as higher oil prices threaten progress.

item.title: UK Lags Rivals in Curbing Influx of Cheap Parcels From China
item.description: More cheap parcels exported from China are flowing into the UK than rival European economies, undermining British retailers that want the government to accelerate a promised crackdown.

item.title: UK Economy Shrank in April as Iran War Started to Take Toll
item.description: The UK economy contracted for the first time in eight months, an early sign of the damage wrought by war in Iran.

item.title: SpaceX Raises $2.2 Billion in Japan Slice of Record-Breaking IPO
item.description: SpaceX raised $2.2 billion from Japanese investors, according to a regulatory filing on Friday, as part of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering.

item.title: Stocks and Bonds Rise on Tumbling Oil Prices
Russians Told There’s Enough Gasoline as Drone War Hits Supply
item.description: Russia’s regional authorities are scrambling to dispel concerns about a gasoline crunch in the wake of Ukrainian strikes on oil infrastructure that sparked reports of shortages at some filling stations.

item.title: Banks Curb Hedge Fund Bets on SK Hynix, Samsung After Rally
item.description: Global banks are curbing hedge funds’ leveraged bets on Asia’s top chipmakers including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co. after a blistering rally this year raised concerns of a potential pullback, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Danske Bank CEO Eyes First Acquisition Since Laundering Crisis
item.description: Danske Bank A/S Chief Executive Officer Carsten Egeriis says it’s time to start thinking about acquisitions, potentially ending a nearly decade-long hiatus from major deals for Denmark’s largest lender.

item.title: Bayer Shares Hinge On Two Court Decisions After $60 Billion Rout
item.description: Bayer AG shares are facing a make-or-break few weeks, with two upcoming court developments key to stemming a more than $60 billion wipeout in market value since the German company’s ill-fated purchase of Monsanto eight years ago.
